title: Delete ALL Your Indicators, and Use Only THIS One! [Perfect for Scalping]
author: SoheilPKO
summary_bullets: This indicator is designed for scalpers.
Alright, on TradingView Platform here, we click on indicators and we search scalping pullback, this one which is written by just uncle L.
Alright, this is the indicator and as you can see it's overlaid on the price chart.
Let me explain what we have here and then I will teach you the trading strategy.
This blue line here is an EMA with length of 200.

We have this green line here which is an EMA length of 89 and this is our fast EMA and then we have this red line here with the two bands above and below that and this is an EMA with length of 34 which is based on close, high and low and this is called price action channel.
You can also have another EMA with an extreme length for identifying major trend of the market.

If we go to the settings of the indicator, here we can turn on the slow EMA which is an EMA with length of 600 and you can see that this gray line here is added to the chart which is an EMA with length of 600 and this is our slow EMA.

So this gray line is slow EMA, the blue line, medium EMA, the green line, fast EMA and this red line with the bands is price action channel which is an EMA with length of 34 based on close, low and high.
All right now let me teach you how you can find buy sell signals based on this indicator.

extracted_rules: If we go to the settings of the indicator, here we can turn on the slow EMA which is an EMA with length of 600 and you can see that this gray line here is added to the chart which is an EMA with length of 600 and this is our slow EMA.
So here the trend is identified and we are in an uptrend and we should look for long position opportunities and the best opportunity is when there is a pullback.
We want a pullback of the price at least to the price channel and if the price goes lower than this it is okay.
So we could enter a long position at this point with the stop plus below the channel and profit target at 1.5 risk to reward ratio.
Deeper pullbacks can give you more probability if you have proper confirmation.
This is a deeper pullback in compared to the previous one and when we check RSI we can see that RSI line went below the center line.

When the RSI line closes back above the center line and also above the moving average line for more confirmation which is the case here this can tell us that the pullback ended and there is a high potential for the price to advance more.

In this case you can see that this scandal also closed above the price action channel and you can see that the indicator also showed this arrow which in times that there is a deeper pullback and then when the price turns to the direction of the trend it shows these arrows.
So here the buy signal is confirmed and in practice we could enter a long position at this point with the stop loss below the channel and profit target at 1.5 risk to reward ratio.
The pullback only means when before that like this case here the price is above the channel clearly.
We only take a trade when there is a clear trend in the market.
I explained how you identify a trend when the price is in an uptrend and now let me teach you a short position setup.
When the price is below the price action channel, the channel is below the green EMA and the green EMA is below the blue EMA.

We want a pullback of the price at least to the price action channel and if it goes higher it is okay as long as the conditions of our first step are not broken.

At this point you can see that RSI line is closed back below the center line and also it is closed below its moving average line and here you can see that this candle is also closed below the price action channel so the sell signal here is confirmed and in practice we could enter a short position at this point with the stop plus above the channel or above the pullback and profit target at 1.5 risk to reward ratio.
